mission.title: kong: kong migrations bootstrap failure with postgres 14
mission.description: kong migrations bootstrap failure with postgres 14. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify kong troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kong deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kong configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kong configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl get configmap -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kong
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### Kong version (`$ kong version`)

latest

### Current Behavior

Error: module 'openssl.rand' not found:No LuaRocks module found for openssl.rand
	no field

steps.title: Apply the fix for kong migrations bootstrap failure with postgres 14
steps.description:
I fixed it by installing luaossl library.
It's a workaround for you . I will be working this problem, seems something went wrong with pgmoon.
https://github.com/mayocream/kong-tests/blob/f6396b97e231ee409edf9bb75084dff2237219ab/issues/8259/docker-compose.yml

Add below parts to docker-compose.yml,
```yaml
apk add curl git gcc musl-dev
luarocks install luaossl OPENSSL_DIR=/usr/local/kong CRYPTO_DIR=/usr/local/kong
```

steps.title: Confirm kong migrations bootstrap failure with postgres 14 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kong --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I fixed it by installing luaossl library.
It's a workaround for you . I will be working this problem, seems something went wrong with pgmoon.
https://github.com/mayocream/kong-tests/blob/f6396b97e231ee409edf9bb75084dff2237219ab/issues/8259/docker-compose.yml

Add below parts to docker-compose.yml, restoring normal.
codeSnippets:
apk add curl git gcc musl-dev
luarocks install luaossl OPENSSL_DIR=/usr/local/kong CRYPTO_DIR=/usr/local/kong

> apk add curl git gcc musl-dev
> luarocks install luaossl OPENSSL_DIR=/usr/local/kong CRYPTO_DIR=/usr/local/kong
>

version: "3.8"

services:
  kong-migrations:
    image: kong:2.7.0
    command: kong migrations bootstrap
    environment:
      KONG_PROXY_ERROR_LOG: /dev/stderr
      KONG_PG_USER: kong
      KONG_PG_DATABASE: kong
      KONG_PG_PASSWORD: <REDACTED>
      KONG_PG_HOST: db
    depends_on:
    - db

  db:
    image: postgres:14
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <REDACTED>
      POSTGRES_USER: kong

  kong:
    image: kong:2.7.0
    environment:
      KONG_PROXY_ERROR_LOG: /dev/stderr
      KONG_PG_USER: kong
      KONG_PG_DATABASE: kong
      KONG_PG_PASSWORD: <REDACTED>
      KONG_PG_HOST: db
    depends_on:
    - kong-migrations

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kong installed or the issue environment reproducible.
